actor_claims.actor_label: any person that has made, or is considering making an alternative proposal
actor_claims.quote_text: any person that has made, or is considering making an alternative proposal
the Company receives an unsolicited alternative proposal from a third party

event_claims.actor_label: the Company and its affiliates and their respective representatives
event_claims.description: The merger agreement prohibits the Company, its affiliates and their representatives from soliciting, initiating, encouraging or facilitating alternative proposals or related inquiries, discussions or offers.
event_claims.quote_text:
Under the merger agreement, the Company and its affiliates and their respective representatives may not:

•

solicit, initiate or knowingly encourage or facilitate the making or submission of an alternative proposal

event_claims.actor_label: the Company and its affiliates and their respective representatives
event_claims.description: The merger agreement prohibits the Company, its affiliates and their representatives from participating in discussions or negotiations regarding an alternative proposal or furnishing nonpublic information to a person that made or is considering an alternative proposal, except to notify the person of the non-solicitation provisions.
event_claims.quote_text: participate in any discussions or negotiations regarding an alternative proposal with, or furnish any nonpublic information regarding the Company or its subsidiaries in connection with an alternative proposal to, any person that has made, or is considering making an alternative proposal

event_claims.actor_label: the Company and its affiliates and their respective representatives
event_claims.description: The merger agreement prohibits the Company, its affiliates and their representatives from entering into any letter of intent, agreement in principle, merger or acquisition agreement, or other agreement relating to an alternative proposal, except permitted confidentiality agreements.
event_claims.quote_text: enter into any letter of intent, agreement in principle, merger or acquisition agreement or any other agreement relating to or providing for any alternative proposal

event_claims.description: Before stockholder approval, if the Company receives an unsolicited alternative proposal from a third party that the board judges constitutes or could reasonably lead to a superior proposal, the Company may provide nonpublic information to the third party after entering into an acceptable confidentiality agreement.
event_claims.quote_text: provide nonpublic information to the third party after entering into a confidentiality agreement acceptable under the terms of the merger agreement

event_claims.description: Before stockholder approval, if the Company receives an unsolicited alternative proposal from a third party that the board judges constitutes or could reasonably lead to a superior proposal, the Company may engage in discussions or negotiations with the third party about the alternative proposal.
event_claims.quote_text: engage in discussions or negotiations with a third party with respect to the alternative proposal

event_claims.description: For the board to change its recommendation or for the Company to terminate the merger agreement to accept a superior proposal, the Company must provide Parent written notice describing the superior proposal.
event_claims.quote_text: provide Parent written notice of the board’s intention to make a change in recommendation or to terminate the merger agreement with a description of the terms of the superior proposal

event_claims.description: For the board to change its recommendation or for the Company to terminate the merger agreement to accept a superior proposal, the Company must give Parent its reasons in writing.
event_claims.quote_text: provide to Parent in writing its reasons for making the change in recommendation or for terminating the merger agreement

event_claims.description: For the board to change its recommendation or for the Company to terminate the merger agreement to accept a superior proposal, Parent receives three business days to propose changes, with extensions for material changes in the superior proposal, and the Company must negotiate with Parent in good faith during that period.
event_claims.quote_text: provide Parent with three business days to propose changes to the terms of the merger agreement

event_claims.description: The Company may terminate the merger agreement before the stockholder meeting to accept a superior proposal if it pays Parent a $255 million termination fee before or substantially concurrent with termination and promptly enters into a definitive agreement for the superior proposal.
event_claims.quote_text: at any time prior to the Company stockholder meeting to accept a “superior proposal”

event_claims.description: The Company must pay Parent a $255 million termination fee if the Company terminates the merger agreement before stockholder approval in response to a superior proposal and promptly enters a definitive agreement for that proposal.
event_claims.quote_text: the Company has terminated the merger agreement before stockholder approval of the merger proposal is obtained in response to a superior proposal and promptly after such termination the Company enters into a definitive agreement with respect to such superior proposal
